scope: Pass B full-surface expansion within the Fundamentals/US-market-data product this connector already targets (legacy never covered forex, crypto, real-time quotes/trades, technical indicators, or per-symbol historical aggregates -- those are wholly separate Finage products with their own auth/config surface and are out of scope for this reverse-ETL account-metadata-style connector, not merely deferred). Adds earnings_calendar and ipo_calendar (both verified live: paths return 'permission' errors, not 404, confirming the routes exist; response field names cross-checked against a documented real-world usage sample since Finage's own docs site renders client-side and could not be scraped for an authoritative field list). dividend-calendar and stock-split-calendar endpoints are verified to exist (same live-path check) but their response field names could not be verified from any available source (Finage's JS-rendered docs pages returned generic template content for every URL fetched, no OpenAPI/Postman spec is publicly published, and legacy never modeled these two resources) -- implementing a schema from an unverified guess would violate the schema-as-projection rule (docs/migration/conventions.md section 2), so they are excluded rather than guessed at.

excluded.reason: SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS: endpoint verified live (returns a permission error, not 404) but its response field names could not be confirmed from any available documentation source -- Finage's docs site is client-rendered and returned identical generic content for every URL fetched, no public OpenAPI/Postman spec exists, and legacy never modeled this resource to derive fields from; implementing with guessed field names would violate the schema-as-projection rule
SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS: same as dividend-calendar -- endpoint verified live but no source (docs, legacy, live authenticated response) confirms its actual response field names
real-time last-quote data is the Stocks product's streaming/snapshot surface, a distinct product from the Fundamentals/market-information surface this connector targets; not modeled by legacy
real-time last-trade tick data; streaming/snapshot Stocks product surface, not fundamentals data; not modeled by legacy
historical OHLCV aggregates require symbol+resolution+date-range config this connector's spec does not model; a distinct per-symbol time-series product, not modeled by legacy
full-market real-time snapshot is the Stocks streaming product surface, not fundamentals/market-information data; not modeled by legacy
Finage's own Composio-catalogued action list marks the historical-data endpoint deprecated in favor of /agg/stock
Forex is a wholly separate Finage product (different symbol namespace, currency-pair config) never modeled by legacy, which only ever read US-stock market-information/fundamentals endpoints
Forex aggregates; same distinct-product rationale as /last/forex
Crypto is a wholly separate Finage product (different symbol namespace) never modeled by legacy
Crypto aggregates; same distinct-product rationale as /last/crypto
Technical Indicators is a distinct computed-analytics product surface with its own per-indicator parameter set (period, series type); not modeled by legacy
open/closed market-hours status flag, not a data catalog resource with a stable record shape
the full US-stock symbol/ticker reference list is a static reference dataset, not the market-movers/fundamentals data legacy read
US Treasury bond rates are a distinct fixed-income product surface, not modeled by legacy
macroeconomic country-detail data is a distinct product surface, not modeled by legacy
